meta.title: Source-backed v1 assumptions for DC backbone comparison
meta.warning: This file mixes public-source values with explicitly labeled engineering proxies where public data is not available. Dynamic frequencies are source-backed; dynamic amplitudes are sensitivity cases, not measured universal constants. The advanced architectures use an NVIDIA-style 800 VDC downstream structure, while the 69 kV subtransmission assumption is a user-specified scenario input rather than a direct NVIDIA-published value.

global.base_it_load_mw_note: Reference hyperscale campus anchor. IEA chart notes use 100 MW as the representative hyperscale data center size.
global.electricity_price_per_mwh_note: U.S. industrial average revenue per kWh for January 2026 converted from 9.29 cents/kWh.
global.default_power_factor_note: Engineering assumption for AC conductor current calculations; replace with site-specific measured PF.

load_profile.name: reference_continuous_operation
load_profile.note: Reference full-load year to avoid inventing an unsupported annual load factor.

cases.name: electromechanical_band_reference
cases.frequency_basis: Representative test point inside the 0.1-5 Hz range that PNNL identifies as relevant to positive-sequence stability studies. This is a modeling reference point, not a directly measured universal AI-workload frequency.

cases.name: measured_14_8hz_case
cases.frequency_basis: Measured oscillation frequency reported in a data-center-rich Dominion Energy region. PNNL cites 5-60 Hz as the main EMT-relevant band for forced oscillation studies.

opendss_validation.note: OpenDSS is used as an AC-side quasi-static validation layer for the source-to-PCC feeder boundary. The downstream DC network remains represented by an equivalent load at that AC boundary; this is not an EMT model of the MVDC backbone or the 800 VDC plant.

opendss_harmonics_validation.note: OpenDSS harmonic mode is used as a standardized AC-boundary harmonic-sensitivity scan. A fixed three-phase harmonic current probe is injected at the PCC and the resulting harmonic voltage distortion is compared across scenarios. This is a structural sensitivity study, not a site-specific IEEE 519 compliance assessment or a vendor-specific converter-spectrum model.

mv_lv_transformer.note: Engineering proxy for large transformer efficiency.

double_conversion_ups.note: Approximate 415 V normal-operation efficiency points from the Schneider Galaxy VX technical specifications.

pdu_transformer.note: Engineering proxy for downstream AC transformer / PDU losses.

server_psu_acdc.note: Modern AC-DC shelf benchmark anchored to OCP Delta's >97.5% figure at rated conditions.

grid_side_acdc.note: Engineering proxy for high-power grid-side rectifier efficiency.

hf_isolated_dc_link.note: Anchored to 99% DC-transformer-class literature and used as a proxy for an AC-fed SST isolated stage.

perimeter_69kvac_to_800vdc.note: Engineering proxy for a direct 69 kV AC to 800 VDC perimeter-conversion stage in an NVIDIA-style 800 VDC facility architecture.

rack_node_dcdc.note: Engineering proxy for high-power rack/node DC-DC conversion anchored to modern high-efficiency rack power-conversion practice.

central_mv_acdc.note: Engineering proxy for centralized subtransmission AC/DC front end anchored to the 800 VDC perimeter-conversion concept and high-efficiency power-conversion literature. In this scenario, the subtransmission voltage is user-set to 69 kV.

isolated_dc_pod.note: Anchored to 99% DC-transformer-class literature and used as a proxy for an isolated DC pod / DC transformer.

architectures.name: traditional_ac
architectures.notes: AC remains the dominant campus distribution domain and power quality ownership is distributed across multiple downstream AC interfaces. This baseline is intentionally not modeled as an 800 VDC data-center architecture.

architectures.name: ac_fed_sst_800vdc
architectures.display_name: NVIDIA-style 69 kV AC -> 800 VDC perimeter conversion
architectures.notes: This comparison case is modeled as a direct 69 kV AC to 800 VDC perimeter-conversion path feeding the 800 VDC facility side, which more literally matches the NVIDIA-style architecture direction than the earlier AC-fed pod proxy.

innovation_metrics.dc_native_resource_path: Direct 800 VDC facility-side path, but AC remains upstream of the facility boundary

architectures.name: proposed_mvdc_backbone
architectures.notes: The AC/DC boundary moves to a common subtransmission front end. Downstream distribution is DC-native with an NVIDIA-style 800 VDC facility bus, while harmonics/PF are handled once at the grid interface. In this scenario, the subtransmission backbone voltage is set to 69 kV.

innovation_metrics.dc_native_resource_path: Native fit for BESS, PV, and pod/rack buffering
